text: The fifth generation (5G) of cellular wireless networks will provide considerably higher data rates, an improved end-user quality-of-experience (QoE), a lower latency, and better energy efficiency. This article gives a brief overview of the key emerging technologies behind 5G, their associated research challenges, and some challenges associated with measurement and testing, which all have implications for the next generation of communication standards.
